artifact.content_preview:
From: adm.clarke@ssc-spc.gc.ca
To: alice.tran@ssc-spc.gc.ca; carol.nguyen@ssc-spc.gc.ca; finance.review@ssc-spc.gc.ca
Subject: NGIS Phase 3 — Decision: Timeline and Budget Revision
Date: March 9, 2026

Project Team,

Following the steering committee session on March 8 and consultation
with Treasury Board Secretariat, I am writing to communicate an executive
decision regarding the NGIS Phase 3 project parameters.

DECISION 1 — TIMELINE EXTENSION:
The project completion date is hereby extended to

extracted_tasks.text: Project completion date extended to December 31, 2026.
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: The project completion date is hereby extended to Q4 2026 (December 31, 2026).
The approved project budget is increased to $3,500,000
Treasury Board notification will be submitted by the SSC Finance team.

extracted_tasks.text: Circulate formal amendment to the project charter for signature.
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: A formal amendment to the project charter will be circulated for signature by end of week.

extracted_tasks.text: Update all project documentation, risk registers, and reporting templates.
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: Please update all project documentation, risk registers, and reporting templates to reflect these revised parameters effective immediately.

metadata.subject: NGIS Phase 3 — Decision: Timeline and Budget Revision

{

    "Decommissioning of Sites B and C physical infrastructure",
    "Migration of all server workloads from Sites B and C to Site A",
    "Physical server rack consolidation and cable management at Site A",
    "Environmental systems (HVAC, UPS, fire suppression) optimization at Site A",
    "Replacement and deployment of Cisco Catalyst 9500 series switches across all three sites",

in_scope: Decommissioning of Sites B and C physical infrastructure
Migration of all server workloads from Sites B and C to Site A
Physical server rack consolidation and cable management at Site A
Environmental systems (HVAC, UPS, fire suppression) optimization at Site A
Replacement and deployment of Cisco Catalyst 9500 series switches across all three sites
Network segmentation reconfiguration per updated security zones
BGP routing table migration and validation
Migration of all on-premise Active Directory domain controllers to consolidated infrastructure at Site A
AD schema extension and forest restructuring
Group Policy Object (GPO) consolidation and testing
Integration testing with downstream applications dependent on AD
Weekly status reports to SSC Project Management Office
out_of_scope: End-user device refresh or replacement (laptops, desktops, mobile devices) — addressed under NGIS Phase 4
Application modernization or re-platforming — no changes to application code, architecture, or hosting platform; lift and shift only
GC-Cloud workload migration to public cloud environments (AWS, Azure, GC-Cloud) — addressed under GC-Cloud Wave 4
End-user training and change management beyond project communications — responsibility of client departments
Procurement of new application licenses — software licensing changes resulting from platform migration
Network monitoring tool upgrades — NOC monitoring platform upgrades deferred to a separate initiative
deliverables: D-001: Project Management Plan — Due: October 31, 2024
D-002: Technical Design Document (TDD) — Due: December 15, 2024
D-003: Security Assessment and Authorization (SA&A) Package — Due: January 31, 2025
D-004: Migration Runbook (Sites B and C) — Due: February 28, 2025
D-005: User Acceptance Test (UAT) Plan — Due: March 10, 2026
D-006: Site A Consolidation Complete (Go-Live) — Due: April 30, 2026
D-007: Post-Migration Validation Report — Due: May 31, 2026
D-008: Project Closure Report — Due: June 30, 2026
constraints: Total approved budget is $2,400,000 CAD inclusive of all fees, disbursements, and applicable taxes
Expenditures beyond the approved budget require a formal budget amendment approved by the Director General of IT Infrastructure
Budget variances exceeding 15% require submission to Treasury Board
Go-Live (D-006) must be achieved no later than April 30, 2026
Final project closure date is June 30, 2026 (end of fiscal year 2025–2026)
Extensions beyond June 30, 2026 require ADM-level approval
All work must comply with ITSG-33: IT Security Risk Management — A Lifecycle Approach
All work must comply with the Government of Canada Cloud Adoption Strategy
All work must comply with the Treasury Board Policy on Government Security
All work must comply with the Privacy Act (R.S.C., 1985, c. P-21)
All client-facing deliverables must comply with the Official Languages Act
Scope, schedule, or budget changes must follow the SSC Project Change Management Process
Scope changes require written approval from the Project Sponsor and must be assessed for cost and schedule impact before implementation

llm_calls.content:
```json
{
  "alignment_score": 0.35,
  "drift_type": "scope_expansion",
  "detected_ask": "Timeline extended to December 31, 2026; budget increased to $3,500,000 CAD; addition of middleware remediation ($340,000), DR site assessment and preliminary design ($400,000), extended contract costs ($260,000), and contingency reserve ($100,000).",
  "baseline_reference": "CONSTRAINT: Total approved budget is $2,400,000 CAD inclusive of all fees, disbursements, and applicable taxes; Go-Live (D-006) must be achieved no later than April 30, 2026; Final project closure date is June 30, 2026; Extensions beyond June 30, 2026 require ADM-level approval; Budget variances exceeding 15% require submission to Treasury Board.",
  "suggested_action": "Initiate a formal Change Control process immediately. Although the directive originates from ADM-level authority (satisfying the approval threshold for timeline extensions beyond June 30, 2026), the project manager must: (1) Issue a formal Change Request capturing all four budget line items and the revised Go-Live and closure dates; (2) Validate that the Treasury Board notification submitted by SSC Finance explicitly covers the 45.8% budget variance ($1,100,000 over $2,400,000 baseline), which materially exceeds the 15% TB submission threshold; (3) Assess whether 'middleware remediation' and 'DR site assessment and preliminary design' constitute new deliverables outside the current scope baseline — both appear to introduce work not listed in the IN SCOPE items or deliverables register and must be formally scoped, estimated, and baselined; (4) Update D-001 (Project Management Plan), the risk register, the schedule baseline, and all affected deliverable due dates (D-005 through D-008) to reflect the revised parameters; (5) Circulate the amended project charter for signature as directed and retain executed copies in the project record; (6) Confirm that the DR site assessment does not implicate GC-Cloud or public cloud environments, which remain explicitly OUT OF SCOPE.",
  "reasoning": "This document represents significant scope expansion across three dimensions simultaneously. First, the timeline extension to December 31, 2026 directly contradicts two hard constraints: the April 30, 2026 Go-Live date for D-006 and the June 30, 2026 final closure date. While the baseline does permit extensions beyond June 30, 2026 with ADM-level approval — and this directive is issued by ADM Clarke — the approval is communicated via email rather than through a formal charter amendment, which has only been promised by end of week. The decision is therefore directionally authorized but procedurally incomplete. Second, the budget increase of $1,100,000 (a 45.8% variance) far exceeds the 15% Treasury Board submission threshold; the email acknowledges a TB notification will be submitted but it has not yet been confirmed as approved, meaning the budget constraint remains technically unresolved at the time of this document. Third, and most critically from a scope perspective, two of the four new budget line items — middleware remediation ($340,000) and DR site assessment and preliminary design ($400,000) — introduce work that does not appear anywhere in the IN SCOPE items, deliverables register, or project constraints. Middleware remediation may conflict with the OUT OF SCOPE exclusion of application modernization or re-platforming, depending on what 'middleware' refers to in this context; this ambiguity requires immediate clarification. The DR site assessment is entirely new work with no baseline reference, suggesting it was introduced at the March 8 steering committee and has not been formally scoped. The extended contract costs and contingency reserve are financial consequences of the timeline change and are less concerning from a scope integrity standpoint, but still require formal budget amendment documentation. The alignment score of 0.35 reflects that while the executive authority to make these changes exists within the governance framework, the majority of the financial and scope changes are unbaselined, procedurally incomplete, or potentially contradictory to existing exclusions."
}
```

parsed_json.detected_ask: Timeline extended to December 31, 2026; budget increased to $3,500,000 CAD; addition of middleware remediation ($340,000), DR site assessment and preliminary design ($400,000), extended contract costs ($260,000), and contingency reserve ($100,000).
parsed_json.baseline_reference: CONSTRAINT: Total approved budget is $2,400,000 CAD inclusive of all fees, disbursements, and applicable taxes; Go-Live (D-006) must be achieved no later than April 30, 2026; Final project closure date is June 30, 2026; Extensions beyond June 30, 2026 require ADM-level approval; Budget variances exceeding 15% require submission to Treasury Board.
parsed_json.suggested_action: Initiate a formal Change Control process immediately. Although the directive originates from ADM-level authority (satisfying the approval threshold for timeline extensions beyond June 30, 2026), the project manager must: (1) Issue a formal Change Request capturing all four budget line items and the revised Go-Live and closure dates; (2) Validate that the Treasury Board notification submitted by SSC Finance explicitly covers the 45.8% budget variance ($1,100,000 over $2,400,000 baseline), which materially exceeds the 15% TB submission threshold; (3) Assess whether 'middleware remediation' and 'DR site assessment and preliminary design' constitute new deliverables outside the current scope baseline — both appear to introduce work not listed in the IN SCOPE items or deliverables register and must be formally scoped, estimated, and baselined; (4) Update D-001 (Project Management Plan), the risk register, the schedule baseline, and all affected deliverable due dates (D-005 through D-008) to reflect the revised parameters; (5) Circulate the amended project charter for signature as directed and retain executed copies in the project record; (6) Confirm that the DR site assessment does not implicate GC-Cloud or public cloud environments, which remain explicitly OUT OF SCOPE.
parsed_json.reasoning: This document represents significant scope expansion across three dimensions simultaneously. First, the timeline extension to December 31, 2026 directly contradicts two hard constraints: the April 30, 2026 Go-Live date for D-006 and the June 30, 2026 final closure date. While the baseline does permit extensions beyond June 30, 2026 with ADM-level approval — and this directive is issued by ADM Clarke — the approval is communicated via email rather than through a formal charter amendment, which has only been promised by end of week. The decision is therefore directionally authorized but procedurally incomplete. Second, the budget increase of $1,100,000 (a 45.8% variance) far exceeds the 15% Treasury Board submission threshold; the email acknowledges a TB notification will be submitted but it has not yet been confirmed as approved, meaning the budget constraint remains technically unresolved at the time of this document. Third, and most critically from a scope perspective, two of the four new budget line items — middleware remediation ($340,000) and DR site assessment and preliminary design ($400,000) — introduce work that does not appear anywhere in the IN SCOPE items, deliverables register, or project constraints. Middleware remediation may conflict with the OUT OF SCOPE exclusion of application modernization or re-platforming, depending on what 'middleware' refers to in this context; this ambiguity requires immediate clarification. The DR site assessment is entirely new work with no baseline reference, suggesting it was introduced at the March 8 steering committee and has not been formally scoped. The extended contract costs and contingency reserve are financial consequences of the timeline change and are less concerning from a scope integrity standpoint, but still require formal budget amendment documentation. The alignment score of 0.35 reflects that while the executive authority to make these changes exists within the governance framework, the majority of the financial and scope changes are unbaselined, procedurally incomplete, or potentially contradictory to existing exclusions.
